content:
2 min read
Just now

--

A few months ago, I was doing what many students and beginner developers do: watching endless tutorials, saving resources, and collecting ideas.
I felt like I was learning, but I wasn’t building enough.
Then I started using AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, Gemini, and Perplexity in my daily workflow. Instead of spending hours searching for answers, I could focus on creating projects, solving problems, and learning by doing.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

What surprised me wasn’t that AI could write code or generate content.
What surprised me was how much faster I could turn an idea into something real.
I’ve used AI to:

• Build websites

• Learn new technologies

• Improve project workflows

• Generate content ideas

• Debug code

• Explore startup and product ideas
But I also noticed something else.
Most content about AI focuses on lists of tools, news, or hype.
There are thousands of articles telling you which AI tools are “best,” but far fewer showing you how to actually use them to build something useful.
That’s why I’m starting this publication.
Here, I’ll share:

• Honest AI tool reviews

• Step-by-step tutorials

• Real project breakdowns

• Comparisons between popular AI tools

• Practical workflows for students, developers, and beginners
My goal is simple:
Learn in public and help others build with AI.
I’m still learning, experimenting, and improving every day. This isn’t a publication from an AI expert who has all the answers.
It’s a place where I’ll document what works, what doesn’t, and what I’m building along the way.
If you’re interested in using AI to create websites, apps, portfolios, digital products, or simply become more productive, I hope you’ll join me on this journey.
The best way to learn is to build.
And that’s exactly what I’m going to do.
Thanks for reading.
— Sneha
